title: SEGV after ALTER MODIFY ORDER BY on S3 table
description:
### Describe the bug
Not sure if related to https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/87517 @bharatnc can you look at this?
### How to reproduce
Start MinIO, set the named collection and run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Int) ENGINE = S3(s3, filename = 'file.csv');
ALTER TABLE t0 MODIFY ORDER BY (c0);
SELECT DISTINCT 1 FROM t0; --SEGV
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```

2025.11.05 16:49:11.670665 [ 2400077 ] {} <Fatal> BaseDaemon: Address: NULL pointer. Access: read. Address not mapped to object.
```

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (c1 INT, c2 VARCHAR(255), c3 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO t1 (c1, c2, c3) VALUES (1, 'test', NULL), (2, 'example', 3.14), (3, 'sample', NULL);
ALTER TABLE t1 ADD INDEX idx_order (c1, c2);
SELECT DISTINCT c2 FROM t1 WHERE c3 IS NULL;
CREATE TABLE t2 AS SELECT c1, c2 FROM t1 WHERE c1 > 1;
key_bug_features: ALTER TABLE MODIFY ORDER BY on S3 table
